item.title: Police Launch Manhunt For Harare Man Accused Of Killing His Own Brother After US$50 Dispute
item.description: Brother Wanted After US$50 Dispute Turns Deadly In Harare Police have launched a manhunt for a 30-year-old Harare man who is wanted in connection with the fatal stabbing of his younger brother following an alleged argument over a missing US$50 (about R825). The incident reportedly happened in Sunningdale 2, Harare, on Monday, 20 July 2026, [...] The post Police Launch Manhunt For Harare Man Accused Of Killing His Own Brother After US$50 Dispute appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: GettyImages-2263686210
item.description: Sikandar Raza leads the Zimbabwe T20I side as they gear up for a three-match series against India starting July 23. Among the key changes from their last series against Bangladesh, wicket-keeper-batter Tafadzwa Tsiga has earned his maiden T20I call-up. All-rounder Wessly Madhever #Zimbabwe #Business #ZimbabweEconomy #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Victoria Falls anthem Kasambabezi captures audiences
item.description: Mthabisi Tshuma, THE collaborative track Kasambabezi, a musical tribute to Victoria Falls, is receiving positive reviews across digital platforms while creating new opportunities for the local artistes behind the project. Produced by DJ Ace Kater, also known as Joseph Black, the song brings together emerging artistes, including Smokey P, Dos Pinky, Alka Tin, Madhuku, Uncle Bobos, Obvidoe, Maskal, Criss Ozzy, Muzie, Raaz, J Tonga and Blaq Spider. Each artiste delivers a unique verse, highlighting their talent while celebrating the heritage and beauty of Victoria Falls, one of the Seven Natural Wonders of the World. Project organiser Handsome Handy Sibanda said the team has been encouraged by the reception the project has received since its release. “The project brought together 14 artistes with the aim of celebrating our culture, showcasing the incredible talent that exists in our community and demonstrating what can be achieved when artists unite behind a common vision. “In a relatively short space of time, the music video has surpassed over 3 000 views on YouTube and what has been even more rewarding is the overwhelmingly positive feedback from viewers. The comments on YouTube, as well as the reactions across the individual artists’ social media platforms, have been filled with praise for the quality of the production, the collaboration and the message behind the song, said Handy. He said the project’s impact extends beyond the number of views it has accumulated. “While success is not measured by views alone, the engagement tells us that the project has resonated with people. It has sparked conversations, increased visibility for all the participating artistes and inspired many who have expressed a desire to see more collaborations of this nature. “We believe Kasambabezi is achieving its objectives. It has united artistes, promoted local talent and created a sense of pride within the community. Of course, this is just the beginning. We will continue promoting the song and exploring more opportunities to ensure it reaches an even wider audience both within Zimbabwe and beyond, he said. Beyond celebrating Victoria Falls, Kasambabezi demonstrates the power of collaboration, providing a platform for emerging artistes to showcase their abilities while positioning local talent for greater recognition on the national and international stage. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Homela’s 80th birthday wish: Save grassroots football
item.description: Lovemore Dube VETERAN football coach and Zimbabwe Saints legend Gibson Homela has used his 80th birthday celebrations to make a passionate appeal for greater investment in grassroots football, the revival of his beloved Zimbabwe Saints and reforms to football development in Zimbabwe. Homela, who turned 80 on Wednesday, made the remarks during a birthday celebration organised by his children and attended by football personalities, community leaders and representatives from the corporate sector. At 80, I don’t want gifts for myself. I want a promise for the next generation. A promise that we will take grassroots soccer seriously, said Homela. He also appealed for the revival of Zimbabwe Saints, the club where he made his name as both a player and coach. I want a promise that we will revive our legends like Zimbabwe Saints, said the Chikwata and Zimbabwe Saints legend. Homela said what Zimbabwean football needs now is action rather than words. Journalists, influencers, leaders - please help me carry this message. The best birthday gift you can give me is action. Let’s build the future of Zimbabwean football, together, said Homela. Reflecting on the impact Zimbabwe Saints had on his life, Homela said the club had shaped not only his career but also the identity of many people in Bulawayo. The club gave me, and so many others, an identity. It gave our community Pride. Today Saints are fighting in the First Division, but they are fighting without enough support. I appeal to our business leaders, our corporates, and our Government to back Zimbabwe Saints with sponsorship. Give them the resources to succeed in the First Division. When the Saints rise, Bulawayo rises, said Homela. The veteran gaffer made his debut for Zimbabwe Saints, then known as Mashonaland FC, in 1963 after being identified at Gweru’s Gafa Grounds alongside Aleck Mwanza and the legendary William Sibanda. He also challenged Government to introduce football coaching as part of the curriculum at teachers’ colleges to strengthen grassroots development. Please introduce soccer as part of the curriculum in our teachers’ colleges. Let every student teacher graduate not just as an educator, but as a qualified soccer according to FIFA and Caf standards, said Homela. He said investing in teachers was equivalent to investing in hundreds of children and would have a lasting impact on football development across the country. Homela described the football coaches who attended his birthday celebration as part of his legacy, saying the game had given him purpose and shaped his life. It taught me that our children are the future of this beautiful game. Right now our talent is being left behind. We find them in the streets, in dusty fields, with so much talent and so little structure, said Homela. (via The Sunday News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Sunday News

item.title: Knox Mtizwa targeted by Highlanders, TelOne after returning from South Africa @TelOneZW
item.description: Striker Knox Mtizwa has emerged as a transfer target for both Highlanders and TelOne in the ongoing mid-season window. Sources close to the developments say the forward, who recently returned to Zimbabwe after spending almost a decade playing in South Africa, is keen on securing a move to one of the two Castle Lager Premier [...] The post Knox Mtizwa targeted by Highlanders, TelOne after returning from South Africa appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe’s Brighton Chimene appointed VAR official for WAFCON
item.description: Zimbabwean referee Brighton Chimene has earned another continental assignment after being appointed as a Video Assistant Referee (VAR) for the upcoming Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) in Morocco. Chimene, the reigning Zimbabwe Referee of the Year, also served as a VAR official at the men’s Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) earlier this year. He [...] The post Zimbabwe’s Brighton Chimene appointed VAR official for WAFCON appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Air Zimbabwe isn’t flying to London—they’re just selling tickets on a Spanish charter @nehandaradio
item.description: Direct Harare-London flights are back, but the aircraft, crew and operations belong to Spain’s Plus Ultra, raising questions about claims that Air Zimbabwe has resumed flying to the UK. The post Air Zimbabwe isn’t flying to London—they’re just selling tickets on a Spanish charter appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Nehanda Radio

item.title: Khumalo, Tshuma families at odds over funeral arrangements for UK triple murder victims @nehandaradio
item.description: The family of slain UK-based Zimbabwean mother Nothabo Khumalo has reportedly rejected efforts by Ndodana Tshuma’s relatives to coordinate funeral arrangements, exposing deep divisions after the triple murder. The post Khumalo, Tshuma families at odds over funeral arrangements for UK triple murder victims appeared first on Nehanda Radio. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
